                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.redexAux_zero {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (ds : V) :
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                redexAux ds 0 = 0
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                source
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                @[simp]
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.redexAux_succ {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (ds n : V) :
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                redexAux ds (n + 1) = if redexAux ds n < n then redexAux ds n else if isRedexPair ds n then n else n + 1
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                source

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  𝚺₁-Function₂ redexAux

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  instance GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.redexAux_definable' {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {m : ℕ} (Γ : LO.SigmaPiDelta) :
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  { Γ := Γ, rank := m + 1 }-Function₂ redexAux
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.redexAux_le {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (ds N : V) :
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  redexAux ds N ≤ N

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  First-hit ≤ sentinel — the search result never exceeds its bound.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.redexAux_isRedexPair_of_lt {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (ds N : V) :
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  redexAux ds N < N → isRedexPair ds (redexAux ds N)

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  First-hit is valid — if the search returns a genuine index (< N), it is a valid redex pair.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.redexAux_eq_self_of_no_redex {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (ds N : V) :
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  redexAux ds N = N → ∀ c < N, ¬isRedexPair ds c

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  No-hit sentinel — if the search returns the sentinel N, then no c < N is a valid redex pair (the search exhausted the range).

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.redexAux_found {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (ds N : V) (h : ∃ c < N, isRedexPair ds c) :
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  redexAux ds N < N ∧ isRedexPair ds (redexAux ds N)

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  First-hit found — when a valid redex pair exists below the sentinel, the search returns one.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.redexAux_min {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (ds N c : V) :
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  c < N → isRedexPair ds c → redexAux ds N ≤ c

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  First-hit is MINIMAL — the search returns the least redex code in range: any redex c < N bounds the result from above. (First-hit scans 0,1,2,… and stops at the first redex, so it is ≤ every redex below the sentinel.) The rank-side companion of redexAux_found.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  noncomputable def GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.redexCode {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (d : V) :
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  V

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  The redex code of a chain = least valid redex pair ⟪i,j⟫ over ds = zKseq d, bounded by ⟪lh ds, lh ds⟫. Buchholz Def 3.2 case 5.1's "least such pair (i,j)", now a definable function.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    V

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    The redex's right-reduction index i (tp(dᵢ) = R_{Aᵢ}).

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      V

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      The redex's left-symbol index j (tp(d_j) = L^k_{Aᵢ}).

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        source
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.redexCode_isRedexPair {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {d : V} (h : ∃ c < ⟪LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh (zKseq d), LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh (zKseq d)⟫, isRedexPair (zKseq d) c) :
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        isRedexPair (zKseq d) (redexCode d)

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        Redex code spec — if a valid redex pair exists in range, redexCode d is one.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        The minimal permissible-premise index finder (Buchholz Def 3.2 case 5.2 dispatch index) #

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        When a K-chain zK s r ds is NON-critical (¬ zKCritical s ds, i.e. some premise dᵢ has tp(dᵢ) ◁ s), Buchholz's case 5.2 reduces at the least such i (tp(dᵢ) ◁ Π). This first-hit bounded search permIdxAux — the single-index analogue of redexAux — returns that least i (or the sentinel lh ds if none, i.e. the chain is critical). Its condition iperm (tp (znth ds i)) s is the exact ¬-conjunct of zKCritical.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        source
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        def GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.isPermPrem {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (ds s n : V) :
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        Prop

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        Permissible-premise test: premise n of chain ds is ◁-permissible w.r.t. conclusion s.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        Equations
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        • GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.isPermPrem ds s n = GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.iperm (GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.tp (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds n)) s

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.permIdxAux_eq_self_of_none {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (ds s N : V) :
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  permIdxAux ds s N = N → ∀ i < N, ¬isPermPrem ds s i

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  No-hit sentinel — if the search returns N, no i < N is permissible (chain is critical).

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.permIdxAux_found {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (ds s N : V) (h : ∃ i < N, isPermPrem ds s i) :
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  permIdxAux ds s N < N ∧ isPermPrem ds s (permIdxAux ds s N)

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  First-hit found — when a permissible premise exists below N, the search returns one.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.permIdxAux_le_of_isPermPrem {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (ds s N m : V) :
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  m < N → isPermPrem ds s m → permIdxAux ds s N ≤ m

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  First-hit is LEAST — the returned index is ≤ any permissible index m < N. Since permIdxAux scans low-to-high and stops at the first hit, it never overshoots a permissible premise. The key leastness fact behind permIdx ≤ j₀ (the Buchholz §5.2 selection i ≤ j₀ minimal s.t. tp(dᵢ) ◁ Π).

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  source
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  noncomputable def GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.permIdx {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (d : V) :
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  V

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  The 5.2 dispatch index of a chain = least permissible premise index, sentinel lh (zKseq d).

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  Equations
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  • One or more equations did not get rendered due to their size.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  Instances For
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    source
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.permIdx_lt_of_not_zKCritical {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s r ds : V} (h : ¬zKCritical s ds) :
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    permIdx (zK s r ds) < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds ∧ isPermPrem ds s (permIdx (zK s r ds))

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    permIdx spec, non-critical chain — if zK s r ds is non-critical (some premise permissible), permIdx lands on the least permissible index < lh ds.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    source
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.permIdx_le_of_isPermPrem {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s r ds m : V} (hm : m < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds) (hperm : iperm (tp (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds m)) s) :
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    permIdx (zK s r ds) ≤ m

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    permIdx ≤ j₀ (the Buchholz §5.2 selection bound). The selected dispatch index is ≤ ANY permissible premise index. In particular, when a chain is non-critical in Buchholz's sense — there is a permissible premise at an index ≤ j₀ (the distinguished top of isChainInf) — the repo's globally-least permIdx lands at-or-below j₀, so the original chain's threading/rank (held only up to j₀) RESTRICTS to threading/rank up to permIdx. This is the structural fact that feeds ZDerivation_iCritReplaceReduce_of's hthread/hrank hypotheses in the R-rule replace wiring.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    source
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.permIdx_le_of_exists_isPermPrem {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s r ds j0 : V} (h : ∃ m ≤ j0, m < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds ∧ iperm (tp (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds m)) s) :
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    permIdx (zK s r ds) ≤ j0

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    The selection bound from a permissible premise ≤ j₀ (Buchholz §5.2, wiring). Packages permIdx_le_of_isPermPrem + le_trans: if SOME premise at an index m ≤ j₀ is permissible w.r.t. the conclusion (iperm (tp dₘ) s), then the globally-least permissible index permIdx ≤ j₀. This is the exact form feeding thread_rank_restrict_of_le's i ≤ j₀ argument (with i := permIdx). The orbit must supply the witness ∃ m ≤ j₀, permissible (Buchholz's non-critical case guarantees it; the repo's permIdx < lh ds non-criticality is WEAKER — permIdx may exceed j₀ without this witness).

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    source
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.thread_rank_restrict_of_le {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s r ds i j0 : V} (hthr : ∀ i' ≤ j0, ∀ (B : V), inAnt B (chainAnt ds i') → inAnt B (seqAnt s) ∨ ∃ i'' < i', B = chainAsucc ds i'') (hrk : ∀ i' < j0, irk (chainAsucc ds i') ≤ r) (hij : i ≤ j0) :
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    (∀ i' ≤ i, ∀ (B : V), inAnt B (chainAnt ds i') → inAnt B (seqAnt s) ∨ ∃ i'' < i', B = chainAsucc ds i'') ∧ ∀ i' < i, irk (chainAsucc ds i') ≤ r

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    Threading/rank restriction to a smaller index (Buchholz §5.2 selection bound, wiring piece B). Given the parent chain's threading/rank data held up to j₀ (hthr/hrk, the isChainInf witness conjuncts) and i ≤ j₀ (the selection bound permIdx ≤ j₀ from permIdx_le_of_isPermPrem together with the orbit's permissible-premise-at-≤j₀ invariant), RESTRICT the threading and rank down to i — exactly the hthread/hrank hypotheses the non-Rep replace capstones (ZDerivation_zK_replace_zIall_of / _zIneg_of) consume. Pure le_trans/lt_of_lt_of_le under the binders; the trivial-but-load-bearing glue between the parent isChainInf and the per-case ZDerivation_iCritReplaceReduce_of.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    source
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    noncomputable def LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.permIdxDef :
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    𝚺₁.Semisentence 2

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    permIdx is 𝚺₁-definable — the dispatch index permIdx d = permIdxAux (zKseq d) (fstIdx d) (lh (zKseq d)) composes the already-definable zKseq/fstIdx/lh/permIdxAux. Required so the tag-4 red dispatch can branch on permIdx and stay 𝚺₁.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      𝚺₁-Function₁ permIdx via LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.permIdxDef
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      source

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      𝚺₁-Function₁ permIdx
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      source
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      instance GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.permIdx_definable' {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {m : ℕ} (Γ : LO.SigmaPiDelta) :
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      { Γ := Γ, rank := m + 1 }-Function₁ permIdx

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      iRcrit — the CLOSED iR critical branch (Buchholz Def 3.2 case 5.1) #

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      The redex finder (redexCode/redexI/redexJ) is now a total definable function of the chain, so the critical reduct d[0] = K^{r-1}_Π d{0} d{1} is a closed term: iRcrit d ρ plugs the functional redex indices redexI d/redexJ d (and their ρ-reducts) into iCritReduct. This eliminates the last existential from iR's critical branch — only ρ (the indexed sub-reduct dᵢ[k]/d_j[0], the N1 structural IH) remains abstract. The descent iord_descent_iRcrit_of_chain then composes: (L3.1 redex existence) ⟹ (the finder finds the SAME-or-earlier valid pair) ⟹ (T3.4 1 ≤ r) ⟹ (iord_descent_iCritReduct_object, the Thm-4.2 cut-elim descent on the genuine seqUpdate reduct).

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      source
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      noncomputable def GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.iRcrit {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (d : V) (ρ : V → V) :
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      V

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      The closed iR critical branch (Buchholz Def 3.2 case 5.1): the critical reduct d[0] built from the FUNCTIONAL redex (redexI d, redexJ d) and the abstract premise-reduct supplier ρ. Closed term; no existential.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      Equations
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      • One or more equations did not get rendered due to their size.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      Instances For
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        source
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.iord_descent_iRcrit_of_chain {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s r ds j0 : V} {Tr Fa : V → Prop} {ρ : V → V} (hds : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Seq ds) (hnf : InternalONote.isNF (iotil (zK s r ds))) (hj0 : j0 < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds) (hAj0 : chainAsucc ds j0 = seqSucc s ∨ chainAsucc ds j0 = LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.qqFalsum) (hchain : ∀ i ≤ j0, ∀ (B : V), inAnt B (chainAnt ds i) → inAnt B (seqAnt s) ∨ ∃ i' < i, B = chainAsucc ds i') (hrank : ∀ i < j0, irk (chainAsucc ds i) ≤ r) (hwfR : ∀ i ≤ j0, ∀ (A : V), tp (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i) = isymR A → 0 < irk A ∨ Tr A) (hwfL : ∀ i ≤ j0, ∀ (k A : V), tp (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i) = isymLk k A → 0 < irk A ∨ Fa A) (hperm : ∀ i ≤ j0, iperm (tp (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i)) (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.fstIdx (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i))) (hnperm : ∀ i ≤ j0, ¬iperm (tp (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i)) s) (hdisj : ∀ (A : V), ¬(Tr A ∧ Fa A)) (hFa_rk : ∀ (A : V), Fa A → irk A = 0) (hFa_bot : Fa LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.qqFalsum) (hNF : ∀ (n : V), InternalONote.isNF (iotil (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds n))) (hρlt : ∀ (n : V), InternalONote.icmp (iotil (ρ n)) (iotil (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds n)) = 0) (hρg : ∀ (n : V), idg (ρ n) ≤ idg (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds n)) (hρNF : ∀ (n : V), InternalONote.isNF (iotil (ρ n))) :
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        InternalONote.icmp (iord (iRcrit (zK s r ds) ρ)) (iord (zK s r ds)) = 0

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        THE NUT, on the CLOSED reduct (E-CRUX2 §8.3, case 5.1). Same hypotheses as iord_descent_critical_of_chain, but the conclusion is the descent on the closed iRcrit (zK s r ds) ρ — the redex indices are now redexI/redexJ (the definable finder), not an existential. The proof shows the finder fires: L3.1 supplies an existential redex ⟪i,j⟫ < ⟪lh ds, lh ds⟫, so by redexCode_isRedexPair the finder returns a (least) valid pair, whose isRedexPair data gives redexI < redexJ < lh ds; then iord_descent_iCritReduct_object (Thm 4.2) discharges the descent on the genuine seqUpdate-based reduct. This makes the WHOLE case-5.1 branch a closed definable object: only ρ's N1 facts (hρlt/hρg/hρNF, the structural IH) and the tp/§5 well-formedness (hperm/hnperm) remain upstream.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        source
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.iord_descent_iRcrit_of_redex {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s r ds : V} {ρ : V → V} (hds : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Seq ds) (hnf : InternalONote.isNF (iotil (zK s r ds))) (hr : 1 ≤ r) (hex : ∃ c < ⟪LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh (zKseq (zK s r ds)), LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh (zKseq (zK s r ds))⟫, isRedexPair (zKseq (zK s r ds)) c) (hNF : ∀ (n : V), InternalONote.isNF (iotil (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds n))) (hρlt_i : InternalONote.icmp (iotil (ρ (redexI (zK s r ds)))) (iotil (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (redexI (zK s r ds)))) = 0) (hρlt_j : InternalONote.icmp (iotil (ρ (redexJ (zK s r ds)))) (iotil (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (redexJ (zK s r ds)))) = 0) (hρg_i : idg (ρ (redexI (zK s r ds))) ≤ idg (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (redexI (zK s r ds)))) (hρg_j : idg (ρ (redexJ (zK s r ds))) ≤ idg (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (redexJ (zK s r ds)))) (hρNF_i : InternalONote.isNF (iotil (ρ (redexI (zK s r ds))))) (hρNF_j : InternalONote.isNF (iotil (ρ (redexJ (zK s r ds))))) :
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        InternalONote.icmp (iord (iRcrit (zK s r ds) ρ)) (iord (zK s r ds)) = 0

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        The K-cut descent, parametrized by an EXHIBITED redex (lap 121). The descent computation iord_descent_iRcrit_of_chain' uses its criticality data (hwfR/hwfL/hperm/hnperm/…) ONLY to run inference_critical_pair_of_chain and manufacture a redex pair; the actual ordinal descent (redexCode → iord_descent_iCritReduct_object) needs just (a) a redex pair exists below the finder sentinel, (b) 1 ≤ r, and (c) the six ρ-facts on the two redex premises. This lemma takes exactly those, DECOUPLING the descent from global criticality. Why it matters (the lap-121 stall lever): in the genuinely-open stall case a threaded atom premise breaks hnperm, so the finder route is blocked — but if the redex can be exhibited another way (e.g. from the chain's principal-cut structure directly), this lemma still discharges the descent, IGNORING the atom.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        source
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.iord_descent_iRcrit_of_chain' {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s r ds j0 : V} {Tr Fa : V → Prop} {ρ : V → V} (hds : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Seq ds) (hnf : InternalONote.isNF (iotil (zK s r ds))) (hj0 : j0 < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds) (hAj0 : chainAsucc ds j0 = seqSucc s ∨ chainAsucc ds j0 = LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.qqFalsum) (hchain : ∀ i ≤ j0, ∀ (B : V), inAnt B (chainAnt ds i) → inAnt B (seqAnt s) ∨ ∃ i' < i, B = chainAsucc ds i') (hrank : ∀ i < j0, irk (chainAsucc ds i) ≤ r) (hwfR : ∀ i ≤ j0, ∀ (A : V), tp (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i) = isymR A → 0 < irk A ∨ Tr A) (hwfL : ∀ i ≤ j0, ∀ (k A : V), tp (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i) = isymLk k A → 0 < irk A ∨ Fa A) (hperm : ∀ i ≤ j0, iperm (tp (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i)) (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.fstIdx (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i))) (hnperm : ∀ i ≤ j0, ¬iperm (tp (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i)) s) (hdisj : ∀ (A : V), ¬(Tr A ∧ Fa A)) (hFa_rk : ∀ (A : V), Fa A → irk A = 0) (hFa_bot : Fa LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.qqFalsum) (hNF : ∀ (n : V), InternalONote.isNF (iotil (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds n))) (hρlt_i : InternalONote.icmp (iotil (ρ (redexI (zK s r ds)))) (iotil (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (redexI (zK s r ds)))) = 0) (hρlt_j : InternalONote.icmp (iotil (ρ (redexJ (zK s r ds)))) (iotil (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (redexJ (zK s r ds)))) = 0) (hρg_i : idg (ρ (redexI (zK s r ds))) ≤ idg (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (redexI (zK s r ds)))) (hρg_j : idg (ρ (redexJ (zK s r ds))) ≤ idg (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (redexJ (zK s r ds)))) (hρNF_i : InternalONote.isNF (iotil (ρ (redexI (zK s r ds))))) (hρNF_j : InternalONote.isNF (iotil (ρ (redexJ (zK s r ds))))) :
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        InternalONote.icmp (iord (iRcrit (zK s r ds) ρ)) (iord (zK s r ds)) = 0

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        The nut, with the ρ-hyps WEAKENED to the two redex premises. iord_descent_iRcrit_of_chain states hρlt/hρg/hρNF as ∀ n, but its proof only ever USES them at redexI/redexJ (the finder output). For the concrete ρ = iR2(znth ds ·) the ∀ n form is FALSE (a critical-chain premise's õ can jump up; an atom premise's iR2 is the identity), so this redex-only form is the one the recursive descent can actually discharge. It pins the entire K-case ordinal obligation to SIX facts about the two redex-premise reducts ρ(redexI),ρ(redexJ) — exactly what the redexI I-rule case (iRedDescent_iR_of_tp_isymR) and the redexJ §5 atomic reduct must supply. (lap-121: now a thin wrapper over iord_descent_iRcrit_of_redex, which manufactures the redex via inference_critical_pair_of_chain from the criticality data.)

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        C0 Fixpoint — the system-Z derivation predicate ZDerivation : V → Prop #

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        The one-step rule ZPhi C d ("d is a Z-derivation given its premises lie in C"), mirroring Foundation's Theory.Derivation.Phi (…/Proof/Basic.lean:280) but over Z's five rules — the K^r rule being variadic (its premise sequence ds, each znth ds i ∈ C), with no Foundation precedent.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        This brick is the STRUCTURAL skeleton (premise-membership + the K^r Seq premise-sequence). The sequent well-formedness (IsFormulaSet), the eigenvariable/rank side conditions, and the §5 atomic axioms are refinements layered onto ZPhi later — they strengthen the predicate but do not change the Fixpoint machinery (monotone/StrongFinite re-prove mechanically). With ZPhi, the next bricks form Fixpoint.Construction → ZDerivation := construction.Fixpoint ![] + its case/induction corollaries, which unblock structural induction (isNF (iotil d)), iR well-definedness, and the ⊥-characterization (derivesEmpty). monotone + StrongFinite are proved here as standalone lemmas (they ARE the Construction fields).

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        source
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        def GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZPhi {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (C : Set V) (d : V) :
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        Prop

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        One-step system-Z derivation rule (structural skeleton): d is built by one of Z's five rules with its premise(s) in C.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        Equations
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        • One or more equations did not get rendered due to their size.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          source
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.zphi_monotone {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {C C' : Set V} (h : C ⊆ C') {d : V} :
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          ZPhi C d → ZPhi C' d

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          ZPhi is monotone in the premise set C (a Fixpoint.Construction.monotone field).

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          source
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.zphi_strong_finite {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {C : Set V} {d : V} :
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          ZPhi C d → ZPhi {y : V | y ∈ C ∧ y < d} d

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          ZPhi is strongly finite: every premise of d is < d, so the rule fires already over {y ∈ C | y < d} (a Fixpoint.Construction.StrongFinite field). The K^r case uses Seq.znth + lt_of_mem_rng (znth ds i < ds) then ds < zK s r ds.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          source
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          noncomputable def GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.zblueprint :
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Fixpoint.Blueprint 0

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          Arithmetized 𝚫₁ core for the Z-derivation Fixpoint (mirrors Foundation Theory.Derivation.blueprint). d = candidate code, C = the recursion set (premises so far). The K^r disjunct uses seqDef/lhDef/znthDef for the variadic premise-sequence membership.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.zPhi_definable {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] :
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.HierarchySymbol.Defined (fun (v : Fin 2 → V) => ZPhi {x : V | x ∈ v 1} (v 0)) zblueprint.core
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            source
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            noncomputable def GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.zconstruction {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] :
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Fixpoint.Construction V zblueprint

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            The Z-derivation Fixpoint.Construction (Φ = ZPhi, with the proved monotonicity).

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            Equations
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            • GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.zconstruction = { Φ := fun (x : Fin 0 → V) => GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZPhi, defined := ⋯, monotone := ⋯ }

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Fixpoint.Construction.StrongFinite V zconstruction
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              source
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              def GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZDerivation {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (d : V) :
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Prop

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              The system-Z derivation predicate ZDerivation : V → Prop — the Fixpoint of ZPhi. d is a Z-derivation iff it is built by one Z-rule from premises that are themselves Z-derivations.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Equations
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              • GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZDerivation d = GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.zconstruction.Fixpoint ![] d
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Instances For
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                source
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                noncomputable def LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.zDerivationDef :
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                𝚫₁.Semisentence 1

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                𝚫₁-definability of ZDerivation (the strong-finite Fixpoint definability, mirror Foundation's Theory.Derivation.defined). Needed as the motive-definability for zDerivation_induction-driven proofs that recurse on a 𝚺₁-function of the derivation (e.g. ZDerivation_zsubst).

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                Equations
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                • LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.zDerivationDef = GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.zblueprint.fixpointDefΔ₁
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                Instances For
