                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    source
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.inference_critical_pair_of_chain_reroute {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s r ds j0 : V} {Tr Fa : V → Prop} (hj0 : j0 < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds) (hAj0 : chainAsucc ds j0 = seqSucc s ∨ chainAsucc ds j0 = LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.qqFalsum) (hchain : ∀ i ≤ j0, ∀ (B : V), inAnt B (chainAnt ds i) → inAnt B (seqAnt s) ∨ ∃ i' < i, B = chainAsucc ds i') (hrank : ∀ i < j0, irk (chainAsucc ds i) ≤ r) (hwfR : ∀ i ≤ j0, ∀ (A : V), tp (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i) = isymR A → 0 < irk A ∨ Tr A) (hwfL : ∀ i ≤ j0, ∀ (k A : V), tp (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i) = isymLk k A → 0 < irk A ∨ Fa A) (hperm : ∀ i ≤ j0, iperm (tp (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i)) (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.fstIdx (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i))) (hnperm2 : ∀ i ≤ j0, ¬(tp (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i) = isymR (seqSucc s) ∨ ∃ (k : V) (A : V), tp (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i) = isymLk k A ∧ inAnt A (seqAnt s))) (hreroute : ∀ i ≤ j0, tp (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i) = isymRep → ∃ i' < i, chainAsucc ds i' = chainAsucc ds i) (hdisj : ∀ (A : V), ¬(Tr A ∧ Fa A)) (hFa_rk : ∀ (A : V), Fa A → irk A = 0) (hFa_bot : Fa LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.qqFalsum) :
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    ∃ (i : V) (j : V) (k : V), i < j ∧ j ≤ j0 ∧ tp (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i) = isymR (chainAsucc ds i) ∧ tp (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds j) = isymLk k (chainAsucc ds i) ∧ 0 < irk (chainAsucc ds i) ∧ irk (chainAsucc ds i) ≤ r

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    The generalized redex finder for a re-routing chain (lap 122 — the genuine fix for the threaded-atom stall, Sub-lemmas A+B assembled). inference_critical_pair_of_chain needs FULL criticality hnperm (no premise permissible for the conclusion, incl. its isymRep clause). A threaded atom/Ax¹ leaf is unconditionally permissible (iperm_isymRep), so that clause FAILS — the documented red-stall. This finder REPLACES the isymRep clause of hnperm with hreroute (every isymRep premise ≤ j0 re-routes its succedent to a strictly earlier premise — exactly chainAsucc_threaded_of_leaf's conclusion for the leaf case). It still produces the genuine (R_A, L^k_A) redex pair with 0 < rk A ≤ r, so any chain whose permissibility-failures are all re-routable leaves still drives the K-descent (iord_descent_iRcrit_of_redex), the threaded-atom stall DISSOLVED with no engine surgery.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    Why the "L-axiom caveat" (lap-121 baton) does NOT arise. Step B takes the LEAST left-symbol index j; the cut formula B = A_i is then re-routed to its LEAST source i' via least_number on the (definable) chainAsucc ds · = B. Every such re-route lands at an index < j (i' ≤ i < j), where by minimality of j there are NO left symbols — so the source can never bottom out at an isymLk L-axiom. The least source i' is non-left and non-isymRep (re-route ⟹ smaller source ⟹ contradiction with i' least), hence I_{i'} = R_B. The genuine residual is therefore only hreroute for NON-leaf isymRep premises (chain/Ind nodes), which chainAsucc_threaded_of_leaf does not cover.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    source
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.iord_descent_iRcrit_of_chain_reroute {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s r ds j0 : V} {Tr Fa : V → Prop} {ρ : V → V} (hds : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Seq ds) (hnf : InternalONote.isNF (iotil (zK s r ds))) (hj0 : j0 < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds) (hAj0 : chainAsucc ds j0 = seqSucc s ∨ chainAsucc ds j0 = LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.qqFalsum) (hchain : ∀ i ≤ j0, ∀ (B : V), inAnt B (chainAnt ds i) → inAnt B (seqAnt s) ∨ ∃ i' < i, B = chainAsucc ds i') (hrank : ∀ i < j0, irk (chainAsucc ds i) ≤ r) (hwfR : ∀ i ≤ j0, ∀ (A : V), tp (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i) = isymR A → 0 < irk A ∨ Tr A) (hwfL : ∀ i ≤ j0, ∀ (k A : V), tp (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i) = isymLk k A → 0 < irk A ∨ Fa A) (hperm : ∀ i ≤ j0, iperm (tp (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i)) (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.fstIdx (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i))) (hnperm2 : ∀ i ≤ j0, ¬(tp (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i) = isymR (seqSucc s) ∨ ∃ (k : V) (A : V), tp (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i) = isymLk k A ∧ inAnt A (seqAnt s))) (hreroute : ∀ i ≤ j0, tp (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i) = isymRep → ∃ i' < i, chainAsucc ds i' = chainAsucc ds i) (hdisj : ∀ (A : V), ¬(Tr A ∧ Fa A)) (hFa_rk : ∀ (A : V), Fa A → irk A = 0) (hFa_bot : Fa LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.qqFalsum) (hNF : ∀ (n : V), InternalONote.isNF (iotil (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds n))) (hρlt_i : InternalONote.icmp (iotil (ρ (redexI (zK s r ds)))) (iotil (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (redexI (zK s r ds)))) = 0) (hρlt_j : InternalONote.icmp (iotil (ρ (redexJ (zK s r ds)))) (iotil (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (redexJ (zK s r ds)))) = 0) (hρg_i : idg (ρ (redexI (zK s r ds))) ≤ idg (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (redexI (zK s r ds)))) (hρg_j : idg (ρ (redexJ (zK s r ds))) ≤ idg (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds (redexJ (zK s r ds)))) (hρNF_i : InternalONote.isNF (iotil (ρ (redexI (zK s r ds))))) (hρNF_j : InternalONote.isNF (iotil (ρ (redexJ (zK s r ds))))) :
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    InternalONote.icmp (iord (iRcrit (zK s r ds) ρ)) (iord (zK s r ds)) = 0

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    The stall-free K-cut descent for a re-routing ⊥-chain (lap 122, target #2). The reroute twin of iord_descent_iRcrit_of_chain': it manufactures the redex via inference_critical_pair_of_chain_reroute (the generalized finder, whose hnperm isymRep clause is replaced by hreroute) instead of inference_critical_pair_of_chain, then feeds it to iord_descent_iRcrit_of_redex. So a valid ⊥-chain whose criticality-failures are all RE-ROUTABLE permissible premises STILL descends — the threaded-atom stall dissolved, modulo discharging hreroute (banked for leaf isymRep by chainAsucc_threaded_of_leaf; the chain/Ind isymRep residual is path α in PENDING_WORK.md lap-122). The six ρ-facts are the same redex-premise reduct obligations as the non-reroute wrapper.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    source
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.inference_critical_pair_of_botChain {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s r ds : V} (hZ : ZDerivation (zK s r ds)) (hant : seqAnt s = ∅) (hsucc : seqSucc s = LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.qqFalsum) (hleaves : ∀ i < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds, tp (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i) = isymRep → (∃ (sk : V), LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i = zAtom sk) ∨ ∃ (sk : V) (Ck : V), LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i = zAx1 sk Ck) :
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    ∃ (i : V) (j : V) (k : V), i < j ∧ j < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds ∧ tp (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i) = isymR (chainAsucc ds i) ∧ tp (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds j) = isymLk k (chainAsucc ds i) ∧ 0 < irk (chainAsucc ds i) ∧ irk (chainAsucc ds i) ≤ r

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    Leaf-stall redex existence for a genuine ⊥-chain (lap 122 — the consolidation). A ZDerivation (zK s r ds) with empty antecedent and ⊥ succedent always contains the genuine (R_A, L^k_A) redex pair (with 0 < rk A ≤ r), provided every permissible (isymRep) premise is a LEAF (hleaves, the atom/zAx1 stall shapes). All of the redex-finder's bookkeeping is discharged internally from ZDerivation:

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    • zKValidF (zKValidF_of_ZDerivation_zK) ⟹ isChainInf (j0/threading/rank), own-permissibility, and the §5 principal-formula well-formedness ⟹ hwfR/hwfL (via tp_isymR_pos/tp_isymLk_pos, Tr := ⊥, Fa := (· = ⊥));
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    • hnperm2 is FREE for a ⊥-chain — tp = R_⊥ is impossible (tp_isymR_pos forces 0 < rk ⊥ = 0), and the L-at-Γmain clause is vacuous (seqAnt s = ∅, nothing is in the empty antecedent);
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    • hreroute from hreroute_of_leaves (the leaf branch) + per-premise ZDerivation (zDerivation_zK_inv). So the ENTIRE leaf-stall case (Buchholz Def 3.2 case 5.1, the documented atom/zAx1 red-stall) reduces to the single structural hypothesis hleaves. The chain/Ind (tp_zK/tp_zInd) isymRep residual is the NON-critical case 5.2 (the splice), handled by separate machinery — NOT a gap in this finder.
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    source
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.redex_or_nonleaf_isymRep_of_botChain {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s r ds : V} (hZ : ZDerivation (zK s r ds)) (hant : seqAnt s = ∅) (hsucc : seqSucc s = LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.qqFalsum) :
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    (∃ (i : V) (j : V) (k : V), i < j ∧ j < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds ∧ tp (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i) = isymR (chainAsucc ds i) ∧ tp (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds j) = isymLk k (chainAsucc ds i) ∧ 0 < irk (chainAsucc ds i) ∧ irk (chainAsucc ds i) ≤ r) ∨ ∃ i < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds, tp (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i) = isymRep ∧ ¬((∃ (sk : V), LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i = zAtom sk) ∨ ∃ (sk : V) (Ck : V), LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds i = zAx1 sk Ck)

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    The case-5.1/5.2 dichotomy for a genuine ⊥-chain (lap 123). Buchholz Def 3.2 case 5 splits a chain inference into critical (5.1, a redex exists — eliminate it) vs non-critical (5.2, a permissible premise exists — splice into it). For a ZDerivation ⊥-chain this is now a CONCRETE, PROVED dichotomy: either there is a genuine (R_A, L^k_A) redex pair (case 5.1, the engine should reduce via iord_descent_iRcrit_of_redex), or there is a NON-LEAF isymRep premise — a chain (tag-3) / Ind (tag-4) node (case 5.2, the engine should splice into it, smaller ordinal). Proof: classical case split on the existence of a non-leaf isymRep premise; its negation is exactly inference_critical_pair_of_botChain's hleaves, which then yields the redex. This is the case split the stall-free endgame needs: the documented red-stall (permIdx selects an atom/zAx1 leaf, red d = d) lands in the LEFT disjunct (a redex exists and is ignored by red's mis-selection) — so the descent must be driven by the exhibited redex, not by red's permIdx, on the LEFT, and by the splice on the RIGHT. Neither disjunct is a red-fixpoint.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    The Option-B obstruction, formalized — why the ordinal-faithful iR2 cannot preserve validity #

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    RedSound (iR2 d is a genuine ZDerivation for ZDerivesEmpty d) is FALSE for the current ordinal-faithful iR2. The critical reduct iCritReduct d i j v w is a chain zK (fstIdx d) (zKrank d − 1) ⟨iCritAux d i v, iCritAux d j w⟩ whose premises are themselves chains (iCritAux _ = zK …). Every chain node has tp = isymRep (tp_zK), and isymRep is permissible for every conclusion (iperm_isymRep). But zKValid's criticality conjunct demands every premise be NON-permissible (¬iperm (tp dᵢ) s) — the very hypothesis the L3.1 redex finder (inference_critical_pair_of_chain) needs to force a genuine R/L redex pair to exist. A chain with a Rep-tagged premise therefore can never be zKValid, so the reduct can never descend again, so the iR2-orbit is not descent-closed. (Confirmed against Bryce–Goré, arXiv:2603.00487: their cut_elimination is genuinely validity-preserving — cut_elimination_valid, shape-dispatched on the cut formula — which the ordinal-faithful iCritReduct shadow is not.) The fix is the genuine, validity-preserving reduct; the inversions above are its peeling primitives.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    source
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.not_zKValid_of_zK_premise {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s r ds m s' r' ds' : V} (hm : m < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh ds) (hprem : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth ds m = zK s' r' ds') :
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    ¬zKValid s r ds

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    A K^r chain with any chain (Rep-tagged) premise is never zKValid. The criticality conjunct ¬iperm (tp dₘ) s fails at the zK-premise m (tp_zK ⟹ isymRep, permissible for s by iperm_isymRep). This is the load-bearing obstruction: the reduct iCritReduct's premises are exactly such chains, so it is never a valid critical chain — the ordinal-faithful iR2 is not derivation-valid.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    source
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.not_zKValid_iCritReduct {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (d i j v w : V) :
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    ¬zKValid (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.fstIdx d) (zKrank d - 1) (iCritReductSeq (iCritAux d i v) (iCritAux d j w))

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    The critical reduct is never zKValid (the concrete obstruction at iCritReduct): premise 0 of its chain is iCritAux d i v = zK …, a Rep-tagged chain, so not_zKValid_of_zK_premise applies. Hence ZDerivation (iCritReduct …) cannot be obtained from chain-validity — RedSound fails for the current iR2, and the genuine validity-preserving reduct (Option A) is required.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    The clean RedSound fragment: the I-rules (tags 1,2) #

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    RedSound asks only that the iR2-reduct be a genuine ZDerivation (the end-sequent matching is handled separately by fstIdx_iR2_of_tag_Ind_or_K). For the I-rules iR2 returns the immediate sub-derivation d0 (iR2_zIall/iR2_zIneg), which is a ZDerivation by inversion — so this fragment is unconditional. The I¬ case is Buchholz 14.23 d[0] := d0 verbatim (no substitution); the I∀ case's GENUINE reduct is d0(a/n) but the ordinal-faithful d0 is also a valid derivation (only its end-sequent differs, which RedSound does not constrain). These never arise on a ZDerivesEmpty code (tags 3,4), but a general tag-dispatched RedSound proof reuses them.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    source
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZDerivation_iR2_zIall {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s a p d0 : V} (hZ : ZDerivation (zIall s a p d0)) :
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    ZDerivation (iR2 (zIall s a p d0))

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    RedSound for the I∀ rule: iR2 (zIall …) = d0 is a ZDerivation.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    source
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZDerivation_iR2_zIneg {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s p d0 : V} (hZ : ZDerivation (zIneg s p d0)) :
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    ZDerivation (iR2 (zIneg s p d0))

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    RedSound for the I¬ rule: iR2 (zIneg …) = d0 is a ZDerivation (Buchholz 14.23).

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    source
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.znth_iIndReductSeq_ZDerivation {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {d0 d1 k : V} (h0 : ZDerivation d0) (h1 : ZDerivation d1) (i : V) :
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    i < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh (iIndReductSeq d0 d1 k) → ZDerivation (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth (iIndReductSeq d0 d1 k) i)

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    Every premise of the Ind-reduct sequence iIndReductSeq d0 d1 k = ⟨d1,…,d1,d0⟩ is a ZDerivation when d0,d1 are.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    source
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZDerivation_iR2_zInd_of_zKValid {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s at' p d0 d1 : V} (hZ : ZDerivation (zInd s at' p d0 d1)) (hvalid : zKValid s (irk p) (iIndReductSeq d0 d1 1)) :
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    ZDerivation (iR2 (zInd s at' p d0 d1))

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    Reduction-soundness for the Ind rule, modulo chain-validity of the reduct. iR2 (zInd …) is the chain zK s (irk p) (iIndReductSeq d0 d1 1); its premises are ZDerivations (the Ind premises) and its Seq structure is free, so it is a genuine ZDerivation exactly when the produced chain is zKValid (the Buchholz reduction lemma — the deep residual).

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    source
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.znth_iCritReductSeq_ZDerivation {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {d0 d1 : V} (h0 : ZDerivation d0) (h1 : ZDerivation d1) (i : V) :
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    i < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh (iCritReductSeq d0 d1) → ZDerivation (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth (iCritReductSeq d0 d1) i)

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    Both premises of the critical-reduct sequence iCritReductSeq d0 d1 = ⟨d0,d1⟩ are ZDerivations when d0,d1 are.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    source
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZDerivation_iCritReduct_of {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {d i j v w : V} (ha : ZDerivation (iCritAux d i v)) (hb : ZDerivation (iCritAux d j w)) (hvalid : zKValid (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.fstIdx d) (zKrank d - 1) (iCritReductSeq (iCritAux d i v) (iCritAux d j w))) :
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    ZDerivation (iCritReduct d i j v w)

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    Reduction-soundness for the critical reduct, modulo chain-validity. iCritReduct d i j v w is the chain zK (fstIdx d) (zKrank d - 1) (iCritReductSeq (iCritAux d i v) (iCritAux d j w)); given its two auxiliaries are ZDerivations and the produced chain is zKValid, it is a genuine ZDerivation. The K analog of ZDerivation_iR2_zInd_of_zKValid (premises + Seq free; zKValid + the auxiliaries' validity are the deep recursive residual — Buchholz's reduction lemma).

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    source
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZDerivation_iCritReductG_of {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {s C rOut rIn0 rIn1 ds0 ds1 : V} (haux0 : ZDerivation (zK (seqSetSucc s C) rIn0 ds0)) (haux1 : ZDerivation (zK (seqAddAnt C s) rIn1 ds1)) (hsAnt : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Seq (seqAnt s)) (hCrk : irk C ≤ rOut) (hCUf : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.IsUFormula ℒₒᵣ C) (hssUf : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.IsUFormula ℒₒᵣ (seqSucc s)) (hsaUf : ∀ k < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh (seqAnt s), LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.IsUFormula ℒₒᵣ (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth (seqAnt s) k)) :
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    ZDerivation (iCritReductG s C rOut rIn0 rIn1 ds0 ds1)

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    The genuine critical reduct is a ZDerivation (R1 DISCHARGED — re-point landed). The ZPhi zK disjunct now carries the faithful, criticality-free validity zKValidF, so the chain introduction zDerivation_zK_intro is a theorem and the former hZPhiK residual is gone. Given only the two genuine auxiliaries being ZDerivations of their reduced endsequents Θ→A(d)/A(d),Θ→D (the recursive Thm 3.4(a) — R2, the one remaining residual of the critical case), the recombination iCritReductG is a ZDerivation. Its validity threading is automatic via zKValidF_iCritReductGen; only the cut-rank drop rk(A(d)) ≤ rOut (Thm 3.4(a), banked irk_cut_lt_rank_*) and the conclusion formula-hood are supplied.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    iRcritG — the genuine CLOSED critical branch (Buchholz Def 3.2 case 5.1, on correct endsequents) #

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    The red-analogue of iRcrit: the redex finder redexI/redexJ is total and definable, so the genuine critical reduct is a closed term once the per-premise reduct supplier ρ (= dᵢ[k]/d_j[0], the N1 structural IH) is fixed. Unlike iRcrit (built on the ordinal-shadow iCritReduct, whose iCritAux auxiliaries reuse fstIdx d and so have the WRONG endsequent), iRcritG is built on iCritReductG, whose auxiliaries carry the cut's reduced endsequents Θ→A(d)/A(d),Θ→D with cut formula A(d) = chainAsucc ds i (the succedent of the redex's R-premise i). This is the K-case of the genuine reduct red.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    source
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZDerivation_iRcritG_of {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {d : V} {ρ : V → V} (haux0 : ZDerivation (zK (seqSetSucc (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.fstIdx d) (cutFormula d)) (zKrank d) (seqUpdate (zKseq d) (redexI d) (ρ (redexI d))))) (haux1 : ZDerivation (zK (seqAddAnt (cutFormula d) (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.fstIdx d)) (zKrank d) (seqUpdate (zKseq d) (redexJ d) (ρ (redexJ d))))) (hsAnt : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Seq (seqAnt (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.fstIdx d))) (hCrk : irk (cutFormula d) ≤ zKrank d - 1) (hCUf : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.IsUFormula ℒₒᵣ (cutFormula d)) (hssUf : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.IsUFormula ℒₒᵣ (seqSucc (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.fstIdx d))) (hsaUf : ∀ k < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh (seqAnt (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.fstIdx d)), LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.IsUFormula ℒₒᵣ (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth (seqAnt (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.fstIdx d)) k)) :
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    ZDerivation (iRcritG d ρ)

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    iRcritG is a ZDerivation (R1 done), modulo only R2 — the two genuine auxiliaries d{0} = K^r (seqUpdate ds i (ρ i)) ⊢ Θ→A(d), d{1} = K^r (seqUpdate ds j (ρ j)) ⊢ A(d),Θ→D being ZDerivations of their reduced endsequents (recursive Thm 3.4(a), the structural IH). The validity threading + cut-rank drop are banked (zKValidF_iCritReductGen, irk_cut_lt_rank_*).

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    source
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZDerivation_iRcritGNeg_of {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] {d : V} {ρ : V → V} (haux0 : ZDerivation (zK (seqSetSucc (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.fstIdx d) (cutFormula d)) (zKrank d) (seqUpdate (zKseq d) (redexJ d) (ρ (redexJ d))))) (haux1 : ZDerivation (zK (seqAddAnt (cutFormula d) (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.fstIdx d)) (zKrank d) (seqUpdate (zKseq d) (redexI d) (ρ (redexI d))))) (hsAnt : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Seq (seqAnt (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.fstIdx d))) (hCrk : irk (cutFormula d) ≤ zKrank d - 1) (hCUf : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.IsUFormula ℒₒᵣ (cutFormula d)) (hssUf : LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.IsUFormula ℒₒᵣ (seqSucc (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.fstIdx d))) (hsaUf : ∀ k < LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.lh (seqAnt (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.fstIdx d)), LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.Bootstrapping.IsUFormula ℒₒᵣ (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.znth (seqAnt (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.fstIdx d)) k)) :
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    ZDerivation (iRcritGNeg d ρ)

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    iRcritGNeg is a ZDerivation — the ¬-case (swapped-half) analogue of ZDerivation_iRcritG_of. The SUCCEDENT half d{0} (replacing the L-redex j = redexJ d, conclusion Γ→A(d)) is haux0, and the ANTECEDENT half d{1} (replacing the R-redex i = redexI d, conclusion A(d),Γ→D) is haux1. Delegates to the same ZDerivation_iCritReductG_of with the two half-sequences SWAPPED, matching iRcritGNeg's seqUpdate slots (ds0 = seqUpdate ds (redexJ d) (ρ (redexJ d)), ds1 = seqUpdate ds (redexI d) (ρ (redexI d))).

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    The iterated descent — n ↦ iord (iR2^[n] z) is an infinite ≺-descent #

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    This is the V-internal analog of GentzenCon.gentzenDescent_descends, on the genuine objects (ZDerivesEmpty/iR2/iord in place of the abstract derivesEmpty/R/ord axioms). It is stated against the one remaining InternalZ obligation, reduction-soundness RedSound (that the reduct of a contradiction derivation is again a genuine ZDerivation — iCritReduct/iRInd outputs satisfy ZPhi), supplied as an explicit hypothesis so nothing is axiomatized. Closing RedSound and internalizing the (here external-ℕ) iteration as a 𝚺₁ graph gentzenDescentφ is what discharges the crux-2 deep axiom gentzen_descent_of_inconsistent.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    source
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    def GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.RedSound {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] :
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    Prop

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    Reduction-soundness (the sole remaining InternalZ obligation): the iR2-reduct of a contradiction derivation is again a genuine Z-derivation.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    Equations
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    • GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.RedSound = ∀ (d : V), GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZDerivesEmpty d → GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZDerivation (GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.iR2 d)
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    Instances For
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      source
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.ZDerivesEmpty_iterate {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (hRS : RedSound) {z : V} (hz : ZDerivesEmpty z) (n : ℕ) :
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      ZDerivesEmpty (iR2^[n] z)

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      ZDerivesEmpty is closed under the whole iR2-orbit (external ℕ-iteration), given reduction-soundness.

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      source
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      theorem GoodsteinPA.InternalZ.iord_iR2_iterate_descends {V : Type u_1} [LO.ORingStructure V] [V ⊧ₘ* 𝗜𝚺₁] (hRS : RedSound) {z : V} (hz : ZDerivesEmpty z) (hcrit : ∀ (n : ℕ), zTag (iR2^[n] z) = 4 → zKCritical (LO.FirstOrder.Arithmetic.fstIdx (iR2^[n] z)) (zKseq (iR2^[n] z))) (n : ℕ) :
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      InternalONote.icmp (iord (iR2^[n + 1] z)) (iord (iR2^[n] z)) = 0

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      THE infinite ε₀-descent of crux-2. For a contradiction derivation z (ZDerivesEmpty z), under reduction-soundness the ordinals n ↦ iord (iR2^[n] z) strictly ≺-descend at every step (icmp (·(n+1)) (·n) = 0). An infinite primitive-recursive ε₀-descent — exactly what PRWO(ε₀) forbids, giving the Gentzen contradiction ¬Con(𝗣𝗔) → False once z is produced by the C0.5 bridge.
